rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative selenocyclization of cinnamyl thioether benzimidazoles with diaryl diselenides to give trans-dihydro-benzoimidazo[1,3]thiazines via an anodic ionic (seleniranium) pathway
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) to its radical cation, which fragments into PhSe+ (electrophile) and PhSe•; cathode reduces protons to H2 to balance charge

key_intermediates.name: bridged seleniranium ion intermediate (F) on cinnamyl thioether
benzylic radical (G) from PhSe• addition to alkene
benzylic cation (H) from SET oxidation of G

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh by one electron to a radical cation that fragments to PhSe+ and PhSe•; PhSe+ adds electrophilically to the cinnamyl alkene of 1a to form a bridged seleniranium ion, which undergoes 6-endo nucleophilic ring closure by the benzimidazole nitrogen through a loose SN1-like transition state; deprotonation gives 3aa while cathodic H+ reduction to H2 balances the charge.
rate_determining_step: 6-endo nucleophilic annulation of the benzimidazole nitrogen onto the seleniranium ion via a loose benzylic-cation-like transition state

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa still isolated in 85% yield under standard conditions with 2 equiv TEMPO, indicating radical pathway is not the major contributor

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell (anolyte 1a+2a)
probe_reagents.observation: Exclusive formation of 3aa (82%) when reactants kept only in anodic chamber, showing cathodic reduction is not required

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell (catholyte 1a+2a)
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa not detected, ruling out a purely cathodic initiation

probe_reagents.name: H2O2 oxidative deselenation of 3aa
probe_reagents.observation: Syn-elimination to vinyl thiazine 6 (61%) supports trans-relative geometry of substituents in 3aa

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a and mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Ep/2(1a)=1.26 V, Ep/2(2a)=1.29 V individually; mixture shows waves at 1.28 and 1.66 V; reduction of 2a at -1.18 V; supports anodic oxidation of 2a initiating the reaction

probe_reagents.name: Thiopropargyl benzimidazole 4
probe_reagents.observation: Annulation gives benzoimidazo[1,3]thiazine 5 in 92%, demonstrating generality of the selenocyclization

notes: Authors explicitly disfavor two alternative pathways: (i) a radical pathway via PhSe• addition giving benzyl radical G, which would require SET oxidation to cation H and is ruled out by divided-cell experiments and TEMPO insensitivity; (ii) initial anodic oxidation of 1a to radical cation K followed by 5-exo radical annulation and selenylation, which would give the undetected 5-exo regioisomer 3aa'. Exclusive 6-endo trans-diastereoselectivity is rationalized by a loose, SN1-like benzylic cation transition state retaining stereospecificity of the seleniranium opening.
